metadata.description: Golden cases for pediatric severity scores and clinical assessments
metadata.source: Apgar 1953, Royal College of Physicians NEWS 2012, AAP clinical literature

score_calculations.clinical_scenario: Vigorous term newborn at 1 minute of life
score_calculations.source: Apgar V. Proposal for a new method of evaluation of the newborn infant. Anesth Analg. 1953;32:260-267
score_calculations.notes: Perfect score at 1 minute: all components maximal

score_calculations.clinical_scenario: Newborn with moderate respiratory depression at 1 minute

expected_output.clinical_action: Provide stimulation, oxygen assessment, reassess at 5 minutes

score_calculations.notes: Borderline score requiring intervention and reassessment

score_calculations.clinical_scenario: Severely compromised newborn requiring immediate resuscitation

expected_output.clinical_action: Initiate full resuscitation: airway, breathing, circulation

score_calculations.clinical_scenario: Newborn improving with resuscitation by 5 minutes

expected_output.clinical_action: Continue routine care with close monitoring for complications

score_calculations.notes: Good recovery at 5 minutes, prognostic indicator of normal neurodevelopment

score_calculations.clinical_scenario: Stable 6-year-old hospitalized for routine observation post-op
score_calculations.source: Royal College of Physicians. National Early Warning Score (NEWS). London: RCP; 2012
score_calculations.calculation_method: All vital signs normal: RR 20-30=0, SpO2 >94=0, Temp 36-38=0, SBP normal=0, HR normal=0, Alert=0
score_calculations.notes: All vital signs in normal ranges for age 6 years

score_calculations.clinical_scenario: 8-year-old with mild pneumonia showing early deterioration

expected_output.recommended_action: Increase monitoring frequency, review treatment, consider escalation

score_calculations.source: Royal College of Physicians. National Early Warning Score (NEWS). London: RCP; 2012
score_calculations.calculation_method: RR elevated=1, SpO2 94=1, Temp elevated=1, HR elevated=1, Alert=0; Total=4
score_calculations.notes: Moderate tachycardia and tachypnea with fever suggesting systemic response

score_calculations.clinical_scenario: 5-year-old in septic shock with critical vital sign abnormalities

expected_output.recommended_action: Escalate immediately to ICU, notify senior physician/intensivist

score_calculations.source: Royal College of Physicians. National Early Warning Score (NEWS). London: RCP; 2012
score_calculations.calculation_method: RR severely elevated=3, SpO2 critically low=3, Temp elevated=1, SBP low=1, HR elevated=1, Altered consciousness=1; Total=10 (capped)
score_calculations.notes: Fulminant sepsis: hypoxia, hypotension, tachycardia, altered consciousness - critical care required

tolerance_explanation.rationale: 0% tolerance for score calculations (exact integer results)
factors: Scores are deterministic based on component values
Interpretation categories are discrete and non-overlapping
